rulings.Case Title: CAROLYN BENGTSON VS. SAN FRANCISCO SUPPLY MASTER, INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Cord Investment Company'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Submission To Written Psychological Testing
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 4, 2013, Line 3 (part 2 of 2). Defendant CORD INVESTMENT COMPANY'S Motion to Compel Plaintiff's Submission to Written Psychological Testing (Part 2 of 2) The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: DENIED. The Court's 5/09/13 order granted Defendant's motion to compel Plaintiff' to submit to an independent psychiatric evaluation entailing a 3-hour interview of Plaintiff by Dr. Strassberg. The court denied, without prejudice, Defendant's request to compel Plaintiff to submit to the MMPI-2 and MCMI-III written psychiatric tests, holding that Defendant had not demonstrated good cause to compel Plaintiff to submit to the tests. A showing of "good cause" requires the party to produce specific facts justifying discovery and that the inquiry be relevant to the subject matter of the action or reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. Vinson v. Sup.Ct. (1987) 43 Cal.3d 833, 840. In connection with the previous, Plaintiff had submitted evidence in the form of the declaration of her treating psychologist Bertagnolli that the tests Dr. Strassberg wishes to administer are used where there is a suspicion of secondary gain and pain complaints in excess of what would be expected given objective physical findings, and he had not observed behaviors that would raise the suspicion of secondary gain motivation (Bertagnolli 4/15 Decl. 12). Defendant submitted no evidence in connection with the prior hearing to justify administration of psychological tests aimed at these issues. Defendant has, with its new motion, submitted a declaration of Dr. Strassberg dated 5/13 (Emmett Decl., Exhibit H). However, Dr. Strassberg's declaration again provides no specific facts concerning this plaintiff or her medical record that would justify administration of psychological tests aimed at these issues. Dr. Strassberg's 5/13 declaration merely states that the MMPI-2 test is the most widely used and widely researched test of adult psychopathology; that the MCMI-III assists clinical psychiatric diagnosis by taking into account personality style and coping behavior; and that it is "standard psychiatric procedure in the community to conduct these psychometric tests." (Strassberg Decl. 7). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: EDDIE VALADEZ VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 4, 2013, Line 17, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Denied as to Defendant's motion for summary judgment and summary adjudication as to the first and second causes of action. As to cause of the first cause of action, Defendant shifted its initial burden of production by showing that there is no triable issue of fact regarding Defendant's response to the images placed on Plaintiff's locker before May 13, 2011. Defendant points to emails Supervisor Wolfgang sent out on April 14 and 28, 2011, ordering SFMTA employees to stop posting photographs which could be deemed harassing. (See Wolfgang Declaration 10-17.) However, Plaintiff has produced evidence that there is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether Defendant supervisors knew or should have known of the harassing conduct and failed to take immediate and appropriate corrective action concerning the racial images that were placed on Plaintiff's locker after May 13, 2011. (See Cal. Gov. Code § 12940(j)(1).) There is a triable issue of fact whether after May 13, 2011 Supervisor Wolfgang took immediate and appropriate corrective action upon receiving a letter from Plaintiff which detailed racial images that had been placed on Plaintiff's locker, and the approximate dates when they had been posted. (See Declaration of Bracamontes in opposition to motion, 6, Exh. D, Ex. 17.) As to the second cause of action, a triable issue of fact exists whether Supervisors Wolfgang and Brewster failed to take all reasonable steps to prevent Plaintiff's racial harassment by directing Plaintiff to the EEO office, instead of reporting the harassment claims themselves or taking other corrective action. (See Cal. Gov. Code § 12940(k).) Pursuant to the Request for Dismissal filed by Plaintiff on May 14, 2013, the third, fourth, fifth and sixth causes of action were dismissed without prejudice. As such, they are moot. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: MARK BIRKHEAD VS. OLIVER PARKER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 4, 2013, Line 6, DEFENDANTS OLIVER PARKER, MICROSOFT CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Demurrer overruled as to the first, second, fourth and fifth causes of action. Sustained, with 10 days leave to amend, as to the third cause of action against Microsoft Corporation. Sustained, without leave to amend, as to second cause of action against Mr. Parker. For the first cause of action, threatening to destroy a career, as alleged, is not within routine personnel management activity. (Yurick v. Sup.Ct. (1989) 209 Cal.App.3d 1116, 1128.) The claim is not clearly preempted by workers compensation exclusivity. For the second cause of action, Plaintiff has pled a number of acts and statutory bases that implicate fundamental public policy concerns against Microsoft. (Foley v. Interactive Data Corp. (1988) 47 Cal.3d 654, 669.) A Tameny tort claim cannot be maintained against individual supervisory employees like Mr. Parker. (Reno v. Baird (1998) 18 Cal.4th 640, 663.) For the third cause of action, Plaintiff must allege "the names of the persons who made the allegedly fraudulent representations, their authority to speak, to whom they spoke, what they said or wrote, and when it was said or written." (Tarmann v. State Farm Mut. Auto. Ins. Co. (1991) 2 Cal.App.4th 153, 157.) For the fourth cause of action, the defamation claim is not clearly barred by the Statute of Limitations; Plaintiff alleges that the defamation occurred on June 2, 2011 and various other times. (FAC, 53.) The alleged defamatory statement that Plaintiff "is taking money under the table (bribes) from Cisco Systems" is neither vague nor imprecise. (Ibid.) For the fifth cause of action, although conspiracy is not a cause of action, "it is a legal doctrine that imposes liability on persons who, although not actually committing a tort themselves, share with the immediate tortfeasors a common plan or design in its perpetration . . . ." (Applied Equipment Corp. v. Litton Saudi Arabia Ltd. (1994) 7 Cal.4th 503, 510-511.) Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to allege a conspiracy. Plaintiff is granted leave to amend as to the third cause of action only. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: ALLIED HEATING & AIR CONDITIONING, INC., A VS. GOTHAM BAY CONSTRUCTION CORPORATION, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Gotham Bay Construction Corp. Provide Further Response To Special Interrogatories, Set One And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 4, 2013, Line 2. Defendant BRANDO EMPIRE SAN FRANCISCO, LLC'S Motion to Compel Gotham Bay Construction Corp. to Provide Further Response to Special Interrogatories, Set One and for Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED. Gotham Bay's specification of documents pursuant to CCP 2030.230 is inadequate because, in response to each interrogatory that is the subject of BESF's motion, Gotham Bay responded by referring BESF to Gotham Bay's entire production of more than 1,700 pages of documents. Such a reference in response to an interrogatory does not provide sufficient detail to permit BESF to locate and to identify, as readily as Gotham Bay can, the documents from which the answer to the interrogatory may be ascertained. Within 30 days of service of notice of entry of this order, Gotham Bay shall serve code-compliant responses to BESF's special interrogatories 4, 8, 11-14, 16-19, 21-24, 26-36, 41-43 and 46. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ERIC NEWBERG et al VS. MARK ISAACS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 4, 2013, Line 7, DEFENDANTS JOEL GOODRICH, VALLEY OF CALIFORNIA, INC. DBA COLDWELL BANKER'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Defendants' demurrer to the third cause of action is overruled. Defendants' demurrer that the third cause of action is barred by the statute of limitations under CCP § 338 is overruled. Plaintiffs have pled sufficient facts to show the delayed discovery rule applies as Plaintiffs could not have discovered that Defendants had made misrepresentations in the Disclosure Statement until they discovered the water damage in November 2010. (See FAC 45-49.) Defendants' demurrer that the third cause of action is barred by the statute of limitations under Cal. Civ. Code § 2079.4 is overruled as Plaintiffs are not challenging the manner with which Defendants inspected the building. Defendants' demurrer that the third cause of action fails to meet the heightened pleadings standard for a cause of action of fraud is overruled. Defendants' demurrer to the third cause of action for uncertainty is overruled because Defendants can reasonably determine from the FAC what issues must be admitted or denied, or what counts or claims are directed against him or her. Defendants' demurrer to the third cause of action for failure to allege sufficient facts to state a claim is overruled. Plaintiffs have alleged that Defendants knew or should have know about the damage to the building because they ordered inspection and has seen or had access to reports from previous inspections that the building had water damage. (See FAC 23.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ASHWIN NANDA, BDA, DDS VS. DR. MICHAEL HACK, DMD, A CALIFORNIA PROFESSIONAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 4, 2013, Line 12, DEFENDANTS DR. MICHAEL HACK, DMD, A CALIFORNIA PROFESSIONAL, DR. MICHAEL HACK, DMD, AN INDIVIDUAL'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Continued to July 1, 2013 for the parties provide courtesy copies. The courtesy copies cannot be located. The parties are instructed to deliver courtesy copies of all papers filed in support of and opposition to the motion no later than of the to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than June 7, 2013 at 4:00 pm. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: DISCOVER BANK VS. MARIA LITINETSKY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 4, 2013, Line 15, PLAINTIFF DISCOVER BANK'S DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT Overruled. The demurrer to the Answer serves no useful purpose. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: L&A WONG FAMILY PROPERTY ASSOCIATION LLC, et al VS. HAROLD WONG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For An Order Compelling Binding Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 4, 2013, Line 16, DEFENDANTS HAROLD WONG, WILLIAM YEE AND ASSOCIATES' Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Compelling Binding Arbitration Continued to July 8, 2013 to give the opposing party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. The opposing party must deliver courtesy copies of the opposition papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than June 4, 2013 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be granted. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the moving and reply papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/MJM)
